feedback_addressed: Resolved GOOGL duplication: GOOGL owned by Communication Services with sector-specific thesis (Search, YouTube, ad tech); Technology report may reference for Cloud/AI but primary coverage lives here
Added 2 SELL recommendations: SNAP and PINS with structural headwinds and regulatory risk
Quantified AI incremental revenue: GOOGL $76-80B (Cloud + Gemini + AI Overviews); META Advantage+ $60B run rate

Modeled DOJ antitrust scenarios for GOOGL with probability weights: Search case (behavioral remedies 55%, stricter 20%, weakened 25%) and Ad Tech case (behavioral 45%, AdX divestiture 35%, full breakup 15%)
Added portfolio allocation weights: GOOGL 22%, META 20%, NFLX 14%, TMUS 14%, DIS 12%, TTWO 8%, SPOT 5%
Added META ex-Reality Labs analysis: core Family of Apps PE at 18.1x excluding $19.2B annual Reality Labs losses
Added correlation analysis: GOOGL-META 0.87 correlation, combined weight capped at 42%, TMUS at 0.15 correlation for diversification
Added SPOT ad revenue decline explanation: Q4 decline was currency-driven, constant-currency +4%, strategic pivot to premium formats
Added content cost analysis: NFLX $20B (39% of revenue), DIS $24B (50/50 sports/entertainment)
Added TikTok regulatory update: ban averted via USDS JV, residual algorithm licensing risk
feedback_ignored: DIS ESPN standalone app TAM analysis still lacks specific subscriber target and break-even subscriber level -- mentioned as key catalyst but not quantified with numbers
TTWO GTA VI downside scenario (50M vs 100M+ consensus copies) is acknowledged as a risk but no explicit EPS impact modeling provided
remaining_flaws: SPOT at 65x PE downgraded to HOLD but still allocated 5% -- the margin expansion timeline is still not quantified with specific dates for when 15% or 20% margins are expected
TTWO at 8% allocation with a single-catalyst thesis (GTA VI) at 45x PE remains a binary bet that could lose 40%+ if the game underperforms
Combined GOOGL + META at 42% is acknowledged as concentration risk but the mitigation is just a cap, not a strategy for what to do if digital advertising enters a downturn
NFLX content cost analysis at 39% of revenue lacks comparison to historical content spend ratio and whether the trend is rising or falling
what_impressed: GOOGL antitrust scenario analysis with 7 sub-scenarios across 2 cases and probability-weighted EPS impact of -$0.35 to -$0.55 is institutional-quality regulatory risk modeling
META ex-Reality Labs PE of 18.1x reframes the investment case: the core ad business is genuinely cheap, and Reality Labs is a call option that currently destroys value
GOOGL sector ownership resolution with explicit statement that primary coverage lives in Communication Services eliminates the portfolio double-count risk
Correlation matrix showing TMUS at 0.15 vs GOOGL/META and combined ad concentration cap at 42% demonstrates portfolio construction awareness
AI revenue quantification with specific dollar amounts (GOOGL $76-80B, Gemini $1.2B subscriptions) moves from v1 vague AI monetization inflection to concrete numbers
SNAP and PINS SELL recommendations add genuine short-side conviction to a portfolio that was previously all long
